## Preserved Original Text


## Entry 007 — March 14, 2026


### Claude Code Revealed What Infrastructure Actually Costs


**How it surfaced:** Session 1 site report showed Claude Code removed 3 files and 272 lines of dead Netlify configuration, found and fixed a silent synthesize 500 error (wrong model slug), and discovered three conflicting domain references (netlify, netlify alternate, vercel) all pointing to different canonical URLs.


**What it reveals:** The site had accumulated technical debt that was invisible from the live URL. The Netlify migration to Vercel was never fully completed — Netlify config was left in place, creating ambiguity in CSP headers, API routing, and canonical URLs. None of this was visible to a human visitor. All of it was silently degrading SEO and occasionally breaking the synthesis function.


**Why this matters theoretically:** This is a Bainbridge Warning specimen within the site itself. The deployment had become reliable enough that the infrastructure debt was invisible until Claude Code did a full codebase audit. The metaphor is almost too precise — the site that describes governance theater was running governance theater in its own deployment layer. Everything looked fine. Several things were broken.


**Content assignment:** This observation belongs in the Eigenform Substack piece as a closing example — the infrastructure of the site instantiating the same decoupling pattern the book describes. Or as a standalone AcheType field note: _"The Site That Described Governance Theater Was Running Governance Theater."_
